title: Review: relay/src/*.rs round 13 security/correctness
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one new P0 auth gap (GROUP_ADD caller membership not verified), two P1 operator footguns (POW_DIFFICULTY silent parse failure, SUBSCRIPTION_DAYS=0 lockout), one P1 subscription policy gap (GROUP_ADD vs GROUP_SEND inconsistency). Core correctness (payment atomicity, PoW replay, diversifier allocation, rate limiting on all msg paths except tracked WHISPER) is solid. The authorization gap in GROUP_ADD is the most significant finding.
Confidence: 20% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) GROUP_ADD authorization gap is a real correctness issue that slipped through previous rounds; there may be other authorization gaps in less-traveled methods. (2) The bus subscriber loop is dead (nie-kef6.16) so cross-instance delivery is untested and could have additional bugs. (3) payment_watcher block stream error handling retries via the inner loop rather than reconnecting the gRPC client — this may cause rapid spinning on persistent stream errors. Would raise confidence if: authorization checks (is_caller_member) were added and tested for all group mutation methods; integration tests existed for payment_watcher with mock lightwalletd.
